title: Slow-cooked Midnight Jamaican Chicken Tagine
description: This slow-cooked chicken tagine is a rich and creamy Jamaican dish featuring cinnamon, bhuna, and a hint of mustard. Perfect for a midnight craving, and no onions or garlic in sight.
instructions: Heat the oil in a large dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chicken and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the cinnamon, cumin, mustard, and bhuna, and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the water and bring to a boil.
Cover the pot and transfer to the oven, where it will cook at 2759 for 2 hours, or until the chicken is tender and falling apart.
Stir in the coconut milk and cook for an additional 10 minutes, until the sauce has thickened slightly.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
Let the tagine rest for 10 minutes before serving.
This allows the flavors to meld together and the chicken to retain its juices.
If desired, serve with steamed rice or roti to soak up the sauce.
